name: aggregator handles file with valid source: header but empty rules: array (no error)
command: bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/aggregator.sh" --root "$PWD/tree" --format json 2>err.txt | node -e 'let s="";process.stdin.on("data",d=>s+=d);process.stdin.on("end",()=>{const r=JSON.parse(s);const fs=require("fs");const stderr=fs.readFileSync("err.txt","utf8");const failures=[];if(r.rules.length!==1)failures.push("expected 1 rule, got "+r.rules.length);if(r.files_processed!==2)failures.push("expected files_processed=2, got "+r.files_processed);if(stderr.length>0)failures.push("unexpected stderr: "+JSON.stringify(stderr));if(failures.length===0){process.stderr.write("ok\n")}else{process.stderr.write("FAIL: "+failures.join("; ")+"\n");process.exit(1)}});'
setup:
cat > tree/google/with-rule.yaml << 'EOF'
source:
  id: g1
  authoritative_publisher: Google
  authoritative_url: https://example.com/g1
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: 2026-05-10T08:00:00Z
  content_hash: sha256:g1
rules:
  - id: only-rule
    name: only
    description: the only rule.
    detector: rubric/detectors/only.sh
recommended_set: [only-rule]
all_set: [only-rule]
EOF

cat > tree/google/empty-rules.yaml << 'EOF'
# Valid file: source header present, rules array intentionally empty.
# Common during scaffolding before any rules are authored.
source:
  id: g2
  authoritative_publisher: Google
  authoritative_url: https://example.com/g2
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: 2026-05-10T08:00:00Z
  content_hash: sha256:g2
rules: []
recommended_set: []
all_set: []
EOF
